key_facts: `sp-teams` is the official Codex-only team/runtime surface; the optional `specify-teams-mcp` facade mirrors status, doctor, live-probe, task listing, auto-dispatch, complete-batch, submit-result, and result-template operations.

Codex team state lives under `.specify/teams/state/` and includes session, dispatch, result, batch, review, task, worker identity/heartbeat/status, mailbox, phase, event, shutdown, claim, config, monitor, and executor records.
Runtime bridge detection prefers `psmux` on native Windows, `tmux` elsewhere, and validates native Windows toolchain availability for psmux/codex/node/npm/cargo/git.

Codex team installation writes `.specify/teams/runtime.json`, `.specify/teams/README.md`, `.specify/config.json`, updates `.codex/config.toml` notify/MCP config when possible, and records restore data.

`auto_dispatch.py` parses task documents, assesses passive parallelism, materializes batches, writes dispatch records, launches workers or the agent-teams batch executor, and completes batches from submitted structured results.

The bundled engine is a private Node >=20 package with TypeScript build script and Rust workspace members `omx-mux`, `omx-runtime-core`, and `omx-runtime`; it includes team runtime, tmux/session handling, worker bootstrap, MCP communication, model contracts, state, notify hooks, prompts, and worker skill assets.
Rust internals were sampled from manifests and representative source; deeper Rust behavior should be re-scanned before changing Rust runtime code.
recommended_atlas_updates: Document the Python CLI/runtime control plane separately from the bundled engine internals.
Document state locations and runtime prerequisite failures.
Mark Rust crate internals as lower confidence unless packeted for a future Rust change.
unknowns: Rust crate internals are sampled, not exhaustively traced.
Runtime behavior depends on external `codex`, `node`, `npm`, `cargo`, `tmux` or `psmux` availability.
